vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/bare soil. P3 (Pothos) is present but severely diminished. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of shared occupant)
inventory_reconciliation.New_Intervention: Introduction of a multi-pin electronic component (IC/header) on the desk surface near the pots.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil and a plant marker; no visible vegetation.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains soil and eggshell fragments; no visible mint.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos is in a state of extreme decline, reduced to a single chlorotic leaf.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears dark and potentially waterlogged in the black pot; yellow pot soil appears dry/compacted.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (likely a calcium/pH intervention).
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a small, multi-pin electronic component (IC) introduced in the final two frames.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Stable, no significant change in plant posture or debris.
temporal_deltas.T-2: Increased shadow/lighting shift; plant remains static.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Introduction of an electronic component to the right of the pots; Pothos leaf remains in a state of terminal chlorosis.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. P1, P2, and P4 are effectively lost. The remaining P3 Pothos is exhibiting severe nutrient deficiency or root rot, indicated by the pale, thin leaf structure. The environment is not currently supporting active botanical growth.
anomalies: Total absence of expected species in P1, P2, and P4
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the original botanical collection. The desk environment has transitioned from a plant-focused setup to one containing discarded organic matter (eggshells) and inorganic electronic components. The sole remaining specimen is a single, struggling Pothos leaf, suggesting the biome has been neglected or subjected to conditions unsuitable for the registered species.
